Patient is an 80-year-old African-American male presenting with worsening shortness of breath and constipation. He also complains of cough and blood mixed with sputum production, but denies fever. Past medical history is significant for asbestos exposure and extensive cardiac history including BPH, PUD, AFib, CAD, Aortic valve replacement (1991, St. Jude mechanical valve #23), ICD implantation, PVD, CABG (1991, 1998), CHF (EF 40%), and asbestos exposure.

O:
Vitals: BP 136/80, Pulse 70, RR 20, Temp 99.3, O2 sat 96% on 2 L NC.
Physical Exam: Elderly male, awake, alert, oriented, no acute distress. Peripheral cyanosis noted. Lungs show bibasilar crackles with decreased breath sounds in the left base. Cardiovascular exam reveals regular rate and rhythm with murmur and metallic click. Extremities show 1+ cyanosis.
Labs: WBC 6.9, Hgb 10.6, Hct 31.2, Plt 160,000. CK 266, PTT 37, PT 34, INR 3.7. Na 141, K 4.2, Cl 111, CO2 23. BUN 18, Cr 1.7. Glucose 91. Ca 8.6. Total protein 6.1, Albumin 3.3. Total bilirubin 1.

A:
Worsening shortness of breath and hemoptysis in an 80-year-old male with significant cardiac history and asbestos exposure.

P:
Continue current medications: Coumadin 6 mg alternating with 9 mg, Prevacid 30 mg daily, Diovan 160 mg daily, Flomax 0.4 mg daily, Coreg 25 mg in the morning and 12.5 mg at night, Aldactone 25 mg daily, Lasix 20 mg daily, Zocor 40 mg daily. Monitor for worsening symptoms.
label: h200-nvl/gemma-4-E4B-it/soap_en
